items.title: Drug charges laid after women allegedly try to evade police after exiting Spirit ferry

children.content: Two women are alleged to have had traffickable quantities of meth and cocaine in their car when they evaded police upon exiting the Spirit of Tasmania ferry in Devonport.

image.altText: A large quantity of illicit drugs and tobacco on a wooden table.

items.title: Half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage released into waterways

children.content: More than half a billion litres of improperly treated sewage were released into the state's waterways in the 2024 to 2025 financial year, including at popular swimming spots such as Launceston's Cataract Gorge.

image.altText: People lounge on the grass next to a natural body of water.

items.title: Neil the seal is back, bigger than ever — and that's a problem

children.content: Neil, an elephant seal well known to Tasmanians, has returned to land, with a new video showing him being coerced off a road for his own safety.

gallery.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.
A large seal chased off a road by a person with a board.

thumbnail.altText: Two people attempt to coax a large seal off a road.
A large seal chased off a road by a person with a board.

items.title: Serpentine leafminer detected in Tasmania for first time

children.content: The insect has been found at a commercial grower in Tasmania's north-west. It damages the leaves of the plants it attacks and can reduce crop yield.

image.altText: A fly-like insect with wings sits on a green leaf.

items.title: Two dead in car crash in Tasmania's south

children.content: Two vehicles collided at Old Beach in Tasmania's south earlier today. The two occupants of one of the vehicles have died at the scene, while the other driver is uninjured.

image.altText: Several police cars and an ambulance seen on a stretch of road that turns a corner, with a river on one side.

items.title: The public can soon see this album hidden away for 126 years

children.content: The Royal Society of Tasmania has owned an album of Owen Stanley's maritime artworks since 1900, but its delicate nature meant it could not be put on display. Now, the society is converting the album into a digital flip book so everyone can see it.

gallery.altText: A watercolour painting of a boat at a jetty, People watch on from the shore.

thumbnail.altText: A watercolour painting of a boat at a jetty, People watch on from the shore.

items.title: LISTEN: Birdlife Tasmania warns that Storm Bay could become a 'bird flu centre'

children.content: The Birdlife Tasmania chair says he fears seabirds circling fish farms in Storm Bay could spread bird flu.

gallery.altText: A black-feathered bird with a while bill that has a greenish tip sitting on a towel.

thumbnail.altText: A black-feathered bird with a while bill that has a greenish tip sitting on a towel.

images.altText: A small group of people stand and talk in front of a white building with large cracks in its exterior at night.
A family holds their dog and prays.
a collapsed building at night - people searching in rubble
people on a sidewalk sitting with dogs on phones

children.content: Two earthquakes struck Venezuela on Wednesday evening, local time, collapsing buildings in the capital of Caracas.

image.altText: rubble and a collapsed building visited by emergency services

children.content: Buildings and houses collapse after a magnitude-7.2 earthquake strikes west of Caracas, leading scientists to estimate "high casualties and extensive damage" in the capital. Follow live.

items.title: Many casualties likely in Venezuela as buildings collapse in Caracas quakes

updates.title: State of emergency declared, airport closed
Mayor confirms fatalities, but toll is unknown
Quakes struck during public holiday, felt 'worse than 1967'

items.title: Hanson's Socceroos own goal should have been a free kick for Taylor

children.content: Pauline Hanson has chosen one of Australia's most popular national sporting brands and visible expressions of cultural diversity in her pitch for a "monoculture".

image.altText: Pauline Hanson stands in front of a blue backdrop. In front of her are two microphones.

items.title: ASX slips while gold futures drop below $US4,000 level

children.content: The unemployment rate has fallen to 4.4 per cent in May, down from 4.5 per cent in April. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A man in a suit, seen from behind, looks at the ASX share price boards.

articles.title: Who is going to qualify for the World Cup knockout rounds?

children.content: The Socceroos will finish either second of third in their World Cup group. Second means qualification for the knockout rounds. Third is a bit more complicated.

image.altText: Aiden O'Neill (left) and Harry Souttar look on

articles.title: Traffic cones on statues and a sea of orange: The traditions taking over the World Cup

children.content: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

image.altText: Norway fans sitting in Times Square doing a Viking row

articles.title: Key Socceroos cog ruled out with injury ahead of crucial Paraguay game

children.content: Wing-back Jacob Italiano is ruled out of Australia's crucial World Cup clash with Paraguay after suffering a groin injury.

image.altText: Jacob Italiano #4 of Australia controls the ball during the FIFA World Cup 2026

children.content: South Africa defeats South Korea 1-0 to lock in a knockout stage spot and leave the Koreans waiting nervously for other results.

items.title: South Africa stuns South Korea to secure World Cup knockout stage spot

updates.title: FT: South Africa stuns South Korea to qualify for next round

items.title: As Ebola spreads, Australia faces rise in 'biblical' disease risks

children.content: The bugs that cause Ebola, hantavirus and diphtheria are microscopic, but many of the barriers to controlling them are human-sized.

image.altText: illustration of 4 people in a web of dots representing how viruses spread

items.title: Olympian arrested at reflecting pool is Trump 'scapegoat', lawyer says

children.content: A lawyer for the Olympian arrested at Washington's reflecting pool says his client is a scapegoat who was targeted to distract Americans from the Trump administration's failures.

image.altText: People gather near the reflecting pool, including a person waving a large American flag.

children.content: Every four years, the World Cup provides the perfect backdrop for fans to showcase their culture's traditional football rituals in front of millions.

items.title: Australians' tricks for dealing with heatwaves that Europeans yet to learn

children.content: Europe's record-breaking heatwave has highlighted its vulnerability to climate change. But countries could look to Australia's experience with scorching summers to learn valuable lessons.

image.altText: Four people playing in water near the Eiffel Tower

items.title: Brisbane Broncos warn of scam offering fake mascot auditions

children.content: A scam is circulating online falsely offering fans a chance to audition to be the Brisbane Broncos' mascot in exchange for payment.

image.altText: The Brisbane Broncos mascot points.

items.title: Taking the plunge in chilly winter water to tackle loneliness

children.content: Young people are turning to local community groups to get out of their comfort zones, make real connections and boost their wellbeing.

gallery.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people swim in an ocean pool at sunrise.

items.title: Ghost nets transformed into art to highlight pollution's 'devastating' impact

children.content: Thousands of tonnes of ocean waste lands on Australia's shores every year. These Queenslanders have found a creative use for some of it.

image.altText: A First Nations man and woman hold a large fish sculpture made from netting

items.title: What pokies addict wishes she knew about gambling

children.content: Experts say the gambling industry is increasingly targeting women, and there are potential risks with a novelty bet.

image.altText: The shadow of a woman's figure on her phone

items.title: France and Vanuatu clash over remote Pacific island where 'god resides'

children.content: Tensions are rising between Vanuatu and France as they negotiate a sovereignty dispute over two volcanic islands, believed to be inhabited by a god.

gallery.altText: A man takes a photo of a volcanic island from the front of a boat.

thumbnail.altText: A man takes a photo of a volcanic island from the front of a boat.

items.title: House prices in two Australian capitals tipped to fall substantially

children.content: House price falls of up to 7 and 8 per cent in Sydney and Melbourne have been forecast for the year ahead, according to Domain. But it is far from doom and gloom for existing owners, with resale profits hitting a record.

image.altText: A sign in front of a building that reads 'Sold'

items.title: Brisbane teen acquitted of planning terror attack targeting Peter Dutton

children.content: During the trial, the prosecution alleged when he was 16, the teen planned and prepared to set off an improvised explosive device at a Queensland Labour Day event in May 2024.

image.altText: Peter Dutton in a room during the campaign.

items.title: As F1 heads to Austria the final result of Monaco goes to the courts

children.content: As F1 descends on the scenic Styrian Mountains for this weekend's Austrian Grand Prix, two teams are in the process of reversing the result in Monaco.

gallery.altText: Pierre Gasly and Oscar Piastri walking on the track, wearing a coat, before an F1 race

thumbnail.altText: Pierre Gasly and Oscar Piastri walking on the track, wearing a coat, before an F1 race

items.title: 86-year-old great-grandmother loves to 'thrash her ute' doing burnouts

children.content: Lorraine "Nan" Tuckett loves nothing more than letting the back end of her ute slide and smelling the rubber burn. She travels around Australia doing burnouts.

image.altText: An older woman waves an orange flag out the side of a black ute with smoke coming out the back of it

items.title: Man accused of former partner's murder denied bail for second time

children.content: Jesse Wahlen Beale, 49, has been denied bail for a second time over the murder of his former partner Crystal Beale, whose body was found in the Brisbane River last February.

image.altText: Crystal Beale smiling.

items.title: The alternative burial method that could soon come to Australia

children.content: Advocates say human composting offers a sustainable, greener alternative to burials and crematoriums, and can also provide a cheaper and equitable option after death.

image.altText: A human compost vessel at recompose facility in Seattle.

items.title: Woman fights to have ex-policeman husband kept behind bars

children.content: A domestic violence survivor speaks out about her fight to keep her former detective ex-husband behind bars as he attempts to be released on parole.

image.altText: A woman with black haair close-up looking sad

items.title: Wildlife carers did not expect to be 'ground zero' for third H5 bird flu case

children.content: A South Australian wildlife rescue group is working with local authorities after Australia's third confirmed case of H5 bird flu was detected in a migratory bird.

gallery.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

thumbnail.altText: A giant petrel bird hides in green seaside foliage at Knights Beach on the Fleurieu Peninsula.

items.title: Senior firefighter claims boss labelled her 'an overly emotional woman'

children.content: A former senior Northern Territory firefighter has claimed the service's deputy chief labelled her "an overly emotional woman" in a complaint to the territory's Anti-Discrimination Commission.

image.altText: A man in a dark blue uniform smiles for the camera.

items.title: Residents in regional Victoria hire security patrols over crime fears

children.content: About 20 residents in the Victorian city of Ballarat are paying a security company to patrol their streets as a "crime deterrent".

image.altText: A car at night with flashing lights.

items.title: Kangaroo Island declared feral pig-free after more than two centuries

children.content: Devastating bushfires provide Kangaroo Island with a unique silver lining as it becomes the largest island in the world to eradicate feral pigs.

gallery.altText: A dark coloured feral pig in bushland

thumbnail.altText: A dark coloured feral pig in bushland

items.title: Small designers hitting roadblocks trying to get support against Shein copies

children.content: Small Australian fashion designers who have had their designs copied by overseas ultra-fast fashion companies say they have hit continuous roadblocks trying to get support.

image.altText: A composite shows a woman in a blue striped dress next to the Shein website selling the same dress.

items.title: Family of 17yo killed in dog attack want mandatory owner licences

children.content: Annalyse Blyton was killed in a dog attack in the NSW Hunter Valley last year. Her family is pushing for changes to dog ownership laws.

image.altText: A man and woman hold a framed photo of Annalyse Blyton. They are wearing jumpers with a pink heart logo.

items.title: 'Heartbreaking': Sydney boarding house to be turned into luxury flats

children.content: A rundown boarding house which was once a haven for vulnerable people in inner Sydney is set to be converted into luxury apartments.

gallery.altText: Beige two-storey apartment building in dim, flat lighting

thumbnail.altText: Beige two-storey apartment building in dim, flat lighting

items.title: Remote community's decade-long wait for clean drinking water

children.content: Residents of Pandanus Park continue their near-daily 600-metre walk to access safe drinking water in a decade-long wait for a solution to dangerous nitrate in their tap water.

image.altText: A woman pushes a wheelbarrow through a barren landscape
